extra.reasoning: The property requires rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b never be jointly true. rec_drug_a is set true only by r2 (priority 50, IF prior_gi_bleed). rec_drug_b is set true only by r5 (priority 50, IF prior_gi_bleed). Both share the identical trigger condition prior_gi_bleed. When prior_gi_bleed=true, r2 fires setting rec_drug_a:=true and r5 fires setting rec_drug_b:=true, making both true simultaneously and violating mutual exclusion. The remaining rules (r1, r3, r4) only affect rec_drug_c and are irrelevant. No higher-priority rule can override these (50 is the max priority and nothing sets rec_drug_a/rec_drug_b to false except the default, which only applies to unset outputs). Counterexample: prior_gi_bleed=true with all other inputs false yields rec_drug_a=true, rec_drug_b=true (rec_drug_c=false by default).
